title: qBraid Establishes QUEST Program to Deliver Unified Cloud-Based QPU Access to 40 Universities - Quantum Computing Report
gist: qBraid launched QUEST, a $200,000-funded workforce program giving 1,200+ students at 40 U.S. universities unified browser-based access to 25+ cloud QPUs from Rigetti, IonQ, AQT, and OQC, coupled with an IRB-approved study measuring whether real-hardware access improves learning outcomes versus simulators.

insights.claim: qBraid's QUEST program pools QPU resources from multiple hardware vendors through a unified browser-based notebook interface, giving over 1,200 undergraduate and graduate students hands-on access and removing the financial and logistical barriers that limit university coursework to classical simulation.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Aggregating multi-vendor hardware behind one interface positions qBraid as a neutral distribution layer; a channel-control play in the quantum stack.
insights.locator: Quantum software and cloud infrastructure provider qBraid

insights.claim: QUEST is backed by a $200,000 pooled fund from qBraid and hardware partners Rigetti Computing, IonQ, Alpine Quantum Technologies, and Oxford Quantum Circuits, providing up to $5,000 in credits per approved course and connecting to 25+ cloud-accessible QPUs across 40 accredited U.S. universities.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Hardware vendors subsidizing student access is a customer-acquisition funnel; early platform lock-in for future quantum developers.
insights.locator: The programmatic rollout is backed by a $200,000 USD centralized fund

insights.claim: QUEST runs an IRB-approved multi-university empirical study collecting anonymous student surveys and platform interaction analytics to evaluate how direct cloud-based QPU access alters measurable learning outcomes versus simulator-only baselines, aiming to establish standardized benchmarks for quantum software instruction.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Standardized pedagogy benchmarks could become the reference for quantum curricula, cementing qBraid's platform influence.

insights.claim: North Carolina A&T State University, under Dr. Raymond Samuel, serves as the anchor academic institution, driving QUEST's core focus on expanding demographic participation across public universities.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Anchoring at an HBCU targets workforce diversification; aligns with federal quantum workforce-shortage concerns.
insights.locator: Headquartered in Chicago and led by CEO Dr. Kanav Setia
